welcome.title: Hi, I'm Maximilian
welcome.text:
I'm looking for an IT internship.

This page isn't a standard resume – I want to show what I can do. Built with HTML, CSS, JavaScript, multi-language support, and everything that comes with it.

Am I a pro yet? No. But I'm motivated, I learn fast, and I really want to get into IT.

Take a look around. Maybe it's a good fit.

aboutSections.title: Security has always interested me
aboutSections.text: Even before I could code, I was fascinated by one question: How do you protect data?

aboutSections.title: The retraining opened up technology for me
aboutSections.text: The retraining introduced me to coding, networks, and hardware – and I love building things.

aboutSections.title: Both together just make sense
aboutSections.text: I want to build systems and understand how to protect them – for me, both belong together.

about.title: About Me
about.text:
Born in Waldbröl, at home in Castrop-Rauxel. Westphalian down-to-earthness meets Ruhr area energy.

From automotive to sales to IT – my path wasn't straight, but it shaped me. At Tecis, I learned to understand people, recognize needs, and find solutions. Even when things don't go as planned.

I'm taking that experience into my new world: technology.

Even before I could code, I was fascinated by one question: How do you protect data? Security has always interested me. The retraining as an IT specialist opened up the whole spectrum: coding, networks, servers – and eventually hardware. A Raspberry Pi here, a temperature sensor there. Building things got me hooked.

And the more I understand about technology, the clearer it becomes: security isn't an extra – it's part of everything. I want to build systems and understand how to protect them – for me, both belong together.

My retraining isn't a change of direction. It's an evolution. I combine service-minded thinking from sales with coding skills to build bridges – between people and technology.

In the nature of Westphalia, while focused on archery or playing guitar, I find the balance that also guides me in programming: patience, precision, and the courage to create something new.

My goal is clear: I want to create digital solutions that are not only technically clean but also humanly meaningful. A synthesis of Westphalian down-to-earthness, digital curiosity, and an eye for security.
